message:
feat(core): add repair flush lifecycle, budget-aware flush daemon, ext4 orphan recovery, btrfs tree block validation, and expanded evidence types

This commit introduces several interconnected improvements across the core
frankenfs crates that collectively advance filesystem repair, write-back
correctness, and observability:

ffs-block (ArcCache / flush daemon):
- Add RepairFlushLifecycle trait to bridge write-back flush notifications
  into the repair pipeline without coupling ffs-block to repair internals.
  The new QueuedRepairRefresh adapter (in ffs-repair) implements this trait,
  queuing block-group symbol refreshes when dirty blocks are durably flushed.
- Introduce budget-aware throttling in the flush daemon: when the Cx poll
  quota drops below a configurable threshold, the daemon reduces its batch
  size (budget_poll_quota_threshold, reduced_batch_size, budget_yield_sleep)
  and logs throttle/resume transitions. This prevents the background flusher
  from starving foreground FUSE operations under heavy I/O pressure.
- Refactor flush_daemon_cycle into smaller methods (effective_flush_batch_size,
  flush_cycle_batch, maybe_log_daemon_resumed) for clarity and testability.
- Add new constructors (new_with_policy_and_repair_lifecycle,
  new_with_policy_and_lifecycles) so callers can compose both MVCC and repair
  lifecycle hooks on a single ArcCache instance.

ffs-core (ext4 orphan recovery):
- Implement full ext4 orphan inode list recovery during mount, mirroring the
  Linux kernel's ext4_orphan_cleanup() behavior. On mount, if the superblock
  has the EXT4_ORPHAN_FS flag set or a non-zero s_last_orphan, the orphan
  linked list is traversed. Inodes with link_count == 0 are fully deallocated
  (blocks freed, inode bitmap cleared). Inodes with link_count > 0 are
  truncated to their recorded i_size via extent tree trimming.
- Add lenient orphan list traversal (collect_ext4_orphan_list_lenient) that
  handles out-of-range inodes, cycles, and unreadable inodes gracefully,
  logging warnings instead of failing the entire mount.
- Add Ext4OrphanRecoveryStats for structured recovery telemetry.

ffs-ondisk (ext4 bitmap verification + btrfs proptest coverage):
- Add verify_inode_bitmap_free_count() and verify_block_bitmap_free_count()
  to detect bitmap corruption by comparing actual free-bit counts against
  group descriptor expectations. These are used by the scrub pipeline.
- Add proptest-based property tests for btrfs parsing: superblock region
  parsing, sys_chunk_array round-trips, header validation with structured
  leaf/internal node capacity invariants, and fuzz coverage for
  parse_leaf_items/parse_internal_items/parse_sys_chunk_array with random
  byte vectors. Add proptest dev-dependency to ffs-ondisk.
- Add similar proptest coverage for ext4 bitmap verification paths.

ffs-repair (evidence types + scrub validators + pipeline refresh queue):
- Add TransactionCommit and SerializationConflict evidence event types with
  their respective detail structs (TransactionCommitDetail,
  SerializationConflictDetail), expanding the evidence ledger to capture
  successful MVCC commits and SSI conflict diagnostics alongside aborts.
- Extend TxnAbortedDetail with read_set_size and write_set_size fields for
  richer abort diagnostics.
- Add BtrfsTreeBlockValidator to the scrub framework: validates btrfs tree
  block headers (bytenr geometry, fsid match) and checksums for metadata
  blocks belonging to the current filesystem, skipping the superblock.
- Implement QueuedRepairRefresh: a flush-driven adapter that maps flushed
  BlockNumber ranges to repair GroupNumbers, queues dirty groups, and provides
  drain_queued_groups() / apply_queued_refreshes() for the scrub daemon to
  consume. This bridges the write-back cache with repair without circular
  dependencies.
- Add budget-aware throttling to ScrubDaemonConfig (budget_poll_quota_threshold,
  budget_sleep) for parity with the flush daemon throttling.

ffs-cli:
- Wire BtrfsTreeBlockValidator into the scrub_validator() dispatch for btrfs
  filesystems, passing fsid and csum_type from the parsed superblock.
- Add print handlers for TransactionCommit and SerializationConflict evidence
  event types in the evidence display path.

Cargo.lock:
- Add ffs-repair dependency to ffs-mvcc, proptest dependency to ffs-ondisk.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
